issues_found: event_type 'monetary_policy_report' is incorrect; this is an educational/informational page, not a substantive monetary policy report
Source is purely educational content explaining basic inflation framework concepts with no substantive policy content
No forward guidance, economic projections, analysis, or market-moving information present
Page content is static informational material rather than a time-sensitive policy document
Low archival value for macro catalyst tracking; contains only publicly available baseline statistics
human_review_reason: The record is factually accurate but the source itself is not suitable for archiving. This is an educational page from the Bank of England explaining their inflation targeting framework—essentially static informational content. It contains no new policy announcements, data releases, analysis, or forward guidance. While the generated record correctly identifies the current Bank Rate (3.75%), CPI (3%), and target (2%), these are publicly available baseline statistics. The page does not provide the substantive content expected of a monetary_policy_report, which would typically include policy analysis, economic commentary, or forward-looking statements. Source should be rejected per guidelines favoring rejection for thin, low-signal sources lacking archival value for macro catalyst research.
